items.title: Agent–environment interaction loop
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  agent["Agent"] -->|"action a_t"| env["Environment"]
  env -->|"state s_{t+1}"| agent
  env -->|"reward r_{t+1}"| agent
items.caption: The canonical RL cycle: the agent acts, the environment responds with a new state and a scalar reward.

items.title: Policy maps state to action
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  state["State s"] --> policy["Policy pi(a|s)"]
  policy --> action["Action a"]
  action --> nextState["Next state s prime"]
  nextState --> reward["Reward r"]
items.caption: The policy is the agent's decision rule. Learning adjusts the policy so actions that lead to higher cumulative reward become more likely.

items.title: RL vs supervised learning data flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  x["Supervised: input x"] --> model1["Model predicts"]
  label["Fixed label y"] --> loss1["Immediate loss"]
  model1 --> loss1
  s["RL: state s"] --> agent2["Agent policy"]
  agent2 --> a["Action a"]
  a --> env2["Environment"]
  env2 --> r["Scalar reward r"]
  r --> agent2
items.caption: Supervised learning compares predictions to fixed labels; RL learns from rewards produced by interaction, which may arrive late and without a single correct action label.
